# Contract and Financial Management

> **Version:** 1.0.0
> **Status:** planned
> **Provenance:** 3P
> **Derives From:** `ddd`
> **Catalog Sources:** `decompositions/netsuite-erp.yaml` (AR/AP primitives)

GENERIC contract and financial management spanning the Agreement and Finance bounded contexts. Standard contract lifecycle (DocuSign/PandaDoc) + accounting (QuickBooks). At <$5M with 3 staff, this is commodity infrastructure — buy off the shelf.

---

## Overview

The only non-trivial aspect is **edition-bound revenue allocation** — mapping exhibitor, sponsor, and ticket payments to financial categories by edition. The offering memorandum confirms strong cash collection velocity: "50%+ cash collected within 4 months of show close." AR aging and collection tracking are the primary financial health signals.

### AR Primitives (from NetSuite ERP decomposition)

These neutral accounting primitives apply regardless of which system (QuickBooks, Xero, manual) handles the work:

| Capability | ERP Primitive | AXPONA Expression |
|-----------|--------------|-------------------|
| AR aging | Accounts Receivable | Exhibitor/sponsor payment aging by 30/60/90 day buckets |
| Cash collection | Payment Application | Track "50%+ within 4 months" metric per edition |
| Dunning | Collections Workflow | Follow-up on overdue exhibitor payments |
| Revenue allocation | Revenue Recognition | Map payments to edition + category (space, sponsorship, ticket, services) |

## Capabilities

- agreement-activation
- edition-revenue-allocation
- invoicing-and-payment-processing
- accounts-receivable-aging
- cash-collection-velocity-tracking
- dunning-and-collections

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Formula | Target |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Cash within 4 months | paid_within_120d / invoiced | >= 50% |
| AR aging median | median days to payment | < 90 |
| Revenue concentration (top 10) | top_10_revenue / total | < 25% |
| Forfeiture rate | forfeited / contracted | < 5% |

## Related Patterns

- `exhibitor-lifecycle-management` — exhibitor contracts are the primary AR source
- `sponsorship-inventory-and-yield` — sponsorship agreements are the secondary AR source
- `financial-health-analytics` — measures this pattern
